title: ActivityConfig
description: Per-activity OnDemand launcher configuration. One file per catalog row at `Config/activities/<id>.json`. Read by `OnDemandActivityLauncher` (docs/Plan/03_PHASE2_ONDEMAND_ENGINE.md S2.7) at the Outfitting / Partying / Travelling / Engaged stages. Field shapes mirror `ActivityDefinition` subtypes in docs/Spec/04_ACTIVITIES.md (HumanJoinPolicy, RoleTemplate, LootPolicy, ReputationStanding) and the `LoadoutSpec` POCO in docs/Spec/17_LOADOUT.md. Casing follows the repo convention (PascalCase) established in `Services/WoWStateManager/Settings/StateManagerSettings.json` and `LoadoutSpecSettings` in `Services/WoWStateManager/Settings/CharacterSettings.cs`.
$comment: S0.10 has shipped docs/Spec/17_LOADOUT.md; the `Loadout` shape below is the JSON projection of `LoadoutSpecSettings` (CharacterSettings.cs:192). Top-level `GearOverrides` and `ReputationOverrides` (per slot S0.11 brief) are activity-level extras the Phase 2 OnDemand launcher merges INTO the per-bot loadout before dispatch â€” see Spec/17 Â§5 'Two execution paths' (test/OnDemand mode applies them offline via `.additem` and `.modify rep`).

ActivityId.description: Catalog row id matching `ActivityDefinition.Id` in `Services/WoWStateManager/Activities/ActivityCatalog.cs` and a row in `docs/Plan/Activities/00_INDEX.md`. Example: `dungeon.ragefire-chasm`.

Loadout.description: JSON projection of `LoadoutSpecSettings` (docs/Spec/17_LOADOUT.md, `Services/WoWStateManager/Settings/CharacterSettings.cs:192`). Applied during the Outfitting stage via `ObjectiveType.APPLY_LOADOUT` (online) or coordinator GM commands (offline). All fields are optional; zero/empty values mean 'don't change' so the spec is additive (Spec/17 overview).

TargetLevel.description: Target character level the bot should reach. `0` = leave as is. `LoadoutTask` issues `.levelup <delta>` last so gear required-level checks already pass.

HonorRank.description: Vanilla PvP rank id (0..14). Offline-only â€” applied by the coordinator via MySQL `UPDATE characters.honor_rank_points`.

MountSpellId.description: mangos `spell_template.entry`. `0` = no mount. `LoadoutTask` issues `.learn <id>` after riding skill is set.

ArmorSetId.description: mangos `item_template_sets` token for one-shot bulk equip via `.additemset <id>`.

SpellIdsToLearn.description: mangos `spell_template.entry` ids granted via `.learn <id>` before anything else.

Skills.description: Weapon / profession skill values applied via `.setskill <id> <value> <max>`. Bot only raises, never lowers.

EquipItems.description: Items the bot must end up wearing. `.additem <id>` + `EquipItemTask`. `InventorySlot=0` means 'server picks'.

SupplementalItemIds.description: Bag-fill items (reagents, ammo, food, bandages) added via `.additem <id>` without equipping.

ElixirItemIds.description: Consumables added then used at end of prep (one `UseItemTask` per id).

FactionReps.description: Faction standings the bot needs (attunement gates, vendor access). Offline-only â€” coordinator runs `.modify rep <factionId> <standing>` pre-login.

CompletedQuestIds.description: mangos `quest_template.entry`s applied offline via `.quest add` then `.quest complete`.

TalentTemplate.description: Named talent build (e.g. `FuryWarriorPreRaid`). Offline-only â€” coordinator runs `.reset talents` then the template's `.add talent <spellId>` chain. See Spec/17 Â§GM-command translation.

LockoutSkip.description: When true, the OnDemand launcher clears the `mangos.character_instance` lockout for each participant (including the human if present) before launch. Allows re-running an instance the human is already saved to. See `LockoutVerifier` (Spec/04 R17).

LevelOverride.description: If non-null, overrides `Loadout.TargetLevel` for participants. Used when the operator wants a level different from the activity's natural band (e.g. running Wailing Caverns at level 60).

GearOverrides.description: Extra item ids `.additem`'d on top of `Loadout.SupplementalItemIds` / `Loadout.EquipItems`. Used for activity-specific consumables, keys, or quest items the launcher should hand out even if the per-bot loadout doesn't mention them.

ReputationOverrides.description: Faction-id â†’ standing-name map. Activity-level rep nudges the launcher applies via `.modify rep <factionId> <standing>` ON TOP OF `Loadout.FactionReps`. Standing names are the `ReputationStanding` enum in docs/Spec/04_ACTIVITIES.md and are translated to the standing-points value by the launcher. Keys are stringified MaNGOS faction ids (mangos.faction.id).

RoleOverrides.description: If non-null, overrides the catalog row's `RoleTemplate` (docs/Spec/04_ACTIVITIES.md `RoleTemplate`). Use to short-staff (e.g. 4-man dungeon) or expand support. Sum must respect `MinPlayers`..`MaxPlayers` on the catalog row â€” validated at launch, not at schema time.

StagingLocation.description: Named-location key resolved at runtime via `Bot/named-locations.json` (S0.12). The OnDemand launcher teleports every participant via `.tele name <bot> <StagingLocation>` at the Travelling stage. MUST match a key in the named-locations seed (asserted by Phase 0 catalog tests). Default convention: same string as the catalog row's `Location` field.

HumanJoin.description: Per-activity human participation rules. Mirrors the `HumanJoinPolicy` record in docs/Spec/04_ACTIVITIES.md (the OnDemand-relevant subset; `HumanCanInitiate` is always true per decision R6).

HumanRole.description: Human's group role. Maps to `HumanGroupRole` enum in Spec/04. `Observer` = human is invited but bots run the show.

BotRaidLeader.description: When true, a bot is raid leader even if `HumanRole=Leader`. Used for raids where the human should follow bot pulls.

GearHuman.description: When true, the launcher also applies `Loadout` to the human player. Otherwise bots are level-scaled to the human.

HumanIdleTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds of human inactivity before the launcher tears the instance down. Default per Spec/04: 300 (5 min) for short activities, 900 (15 min) for raids.

LootPolicy.description: Group loot rule applied at the Partying stage. Mirrors `LootPolicy` enum in docs/Spec/04_ACTIVITIES.md. Note Spec/04: in OnDemand mode loot priority does not really matter because characters are ephemeral; this field is preserved for the Partying-stage `.group settings` GM commands.

TearDownPolicy.description: What the launcher does at the TearDown stage. See docs/Spec/02_STATEMANAGER.md OnDemand stage 6.

Action.description: `DeleteCharacters` = `.character delete` for ephemeral pools (default for dungeons/raids/BGs). `HoldForReuse` = keep characters parked for `HoldTtlSeconds` to reuse on the next request. `LogOutOnly` = leave characters in DB but log out (useful for profession runs that build reputation across instances).

HoldTtlSeconds.description: Only meaningful when `Action=HoldForReuse`. Seconds to keep the characters before deletion. Null for the other actions.
